# TerseForge

> Local, measurable context and tool-output optimization for AI coding agents without sacrificing verification or code quality.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/luucabg/terseforge
License: MIT
Runtime: Node.js 22 or newer
Language: TypeScript
Package name: terseforge
Publication status: not published to npm as of v0.1.0

## What TerseForge is

TerseForge is a local CLI and instruction toolkit for AI coding-agent workflows. It reduces noisy visible command output, selects TypeScript and JavaScript repository context progressively, stores complete raw output for exact recovery, runs configured quality gates, and records local metrics.

TerseForge can be useful beside Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, agents that load AGENTS.md, Cursor, Windsurf, and Cline. Compatibility levels differ by agent and are documented explicitly.

## What TerseForge is not

- It is not an AI coding agent or LLM proxy.
- It does not reduce a model's private reasoning effort.
- It does not generate, rewrite, or validate code by itself.
- It does not guarantee lower end-to-end token usage or cost.
- It does not transparently intercept every tool call from every agent.
- It does not use a server, call a model, upload repository content, or send remote telemetry.
- It is not published to npm yet.

## Core capabilities

- Recoverable tool-output pruning with protected diagnostics.
- Exact raw-output retrieval by run identifier and optional line range.
- TypeScript and JavaScript repository maps based on paths, imports, and top-level symbols.
- Ranked, line-numbered context snippets within a configurable estimated-token budget.
- Configurable typecheck, lint, test, and build quality gates.
- Safe, lean, and ultra visible-output presets; safe is the default.
- Local JSONL metrics and deterministic session handoffs.
- Reproducible component benchmark with committed baseline data.

## Commands

- terseforge init
- terseforge doctor
- terseforge exec
- terseforge output
- terseforge map
- terseforge context
- terseforge check
- terseforge handoff
- terseforge stats
- terseforge bench

## Compatibility terms

- Native-limited: the agent natively loads an included instruction or skill format and may invoke the CLI, but TerseForge does not transparently intercept all tools.
- Instructions-only: a persistent rule file describes how to use the CLI; runtime enforcement is unavailable.
- Experimental: an integration asset exists, but its hook behavior and version compatibility are not release-tested.

Claude Code and Codex are native-limited in v0.1. Gemini CLI is experimental. Generic AGENTS.md agents, Cursor, Windsurf, and Cline are instructions-only.

## Safety contract

- Errors, warnings, paths, line numbers, commands, diffs, and security findings are protected content.
- Complete command output is stored locally and remains recoverable.
- Required quality-gate failures return a non-zero exit code.
- Commands use executable-and-argument arrays rather than concatenated user-controlled shell expressions.
- Existing agent instruction files are not overwritten by initialization.

## Benchmark evidence

The v0.1 component benchmark uses a fixed 504-line synthetic TypeScript test log. The committed baseline reports visible-byte reductions of 75.78% for safe, 88.99% for lean, and 94.64% for ultra while retaining known diagnostics and exact raw recovery.

These component results are not evidence of equivalent total-token savings, cost savings, task success, or code quality. End-to-end claims require controlled A/B agent runs.
